audit_items.proof_trace: All 25+ call sites across the codebase invoke the reference constructor (ScopedLocker(LockType& lock), L17), passing member objects by lvalue reference; C++ guarantees references cannot be null, so _lock is always non-null. The pointer constructor (L23) lacks a null guard before _lock->lock() at L26, but it is dead API — never called anywhere in the repository. No attacker-controlled path exists from NetworkRPC to ScopedLocker(nullptr), as lock objects are always embedded member variables.
